ModMetadata.Name: AIMeE Bug Fixes
ModMetadata.Author: FlorpyDorp
ModMetadata.Description:
This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx to be installed with the StationeersLaunchPad plugin. See: https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad
Fixes fourteen bugs with the AIMeE mining robot:
1. Mine Deadlock: AIMeE stops mining after first ore because _mineCancellation is never reset after Mine() completes. 2. Off-State Teleporting: When turned off but receiving logic, AIMeE hops/teleports because TryUnstuck/PathToTarget have no OnOff guard. 3. Tread Animation Desync: On dedicated server clients, tread UV animations are frozen because WheelRpm is never set on non-authority clients. 4. Roam Regression: AIMeE stops exploring when no ore is in scan range because driving logic is skipped when queue is empty. Also restores MinableSearchArea from 16 to 32. 5. Pickup Launch: Picking up/dragging AIMeE launches the player because wheel motor torque transfers through the drag joint. 6. MineablesInQueue Client Sync: MineablesInQueue always reads 0 on multiplayer clients because the queue is a local server-only list. 7. MineablesInVicinity Overcount: Vicinity count includes ore from entire veins beyond the search area due to missing per-ore bounds check. 8. Carry Collision Launch: Carrying AIMeE and bumping terrain/objects launches the player because WheelCollider suspension forces transfer through the drag joint. 9. Stuck Teleporting: When stuck for 60 seconds, Unstuck() teleports AIMeE by directly writing position. Replaced with physics-based escape (backward impulse + random torque). Also adds velocity-based stuck detection to catch AIMeEs pushing against each other. 10. Distant Ore Selection: AIMeEs mine far-away ore instead of closest. Queue now sorted by distance so nearest ore is mined first. 11. Circling Fix: AIMeEs circle aimlessly due to stuck detection feedback loop. Added post-reverse cooldown, mild steering reset after reversing, and reduced reverse duration. Also reduced queue cap from 10 to 5 for better multi-AIMeE ore sharing. 12. Ghost Ore Fix: Ore mined by AIMeE stays visible on multiplayer clients. Fixed by injecting a VoxelChangeEvent directly into the SyncList, bypassing SetDensityWorldSpace entirely. Zero server-side terrain work. Client receives the event and calls MineAtPositionClient to remove the ore. Works for all surface ore visible to players. 13. Underground Ore Fix: AIMeE targets ore deep underground in tunnels and caves. The surface check (IsNearSurface) only tests a single voxel 3 blocks above the ore. In a tunnel, that point is empty, so underground ore passes the check. Fixed with a column scan that detects cave ceilings — if solid terrain exists above an empty gap, the ore is rejected as unreachable. 14. Stale Queue Fix: AIMeE's ore queue is never cleared when picked up, turned on, or given a TargetXYZ. Stale entries point to ore at old locations and block other AIMeEs via AllAimeeQueuedMinables. Fixed by clearing the queue on pickup, power-on, and TargetX/Y/Z writes.
Debug overlay now shows [SERVER]/[REMOTE] labels and displays client-appropriate data on dedicated servers.
Works on both dedicated servers and clients.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog:
v0.15.3: Bug 12 improved — two-event approach (no terrain holes) - Bug 12: Previous fix (density 0) carved visible holes in terrain on clients - Now uses two VoxelChangeEvents per mine: event 1 (density 0) removes ore, event 2 (original density) restores terrain. Ore gone, terrain intact. - Server terrain still completely unmodified
v0.15.2: Bug 12 improved — fix ghost ore for subsurface mining - Bug 12: Send density 0 in VoxelChangeEvent instead of current density - Ore 1-3 blocks below surface has solid density (>= 127), which failed ShouldReleaseMinables - MineAtPositionClient never fired on clients — ghost ore persisted - Now always sends density 0: client carves terrain + removes ore model (same as player mining) - Server terrain unmodified — only SyncList is touched - Client visual hole is cosmetic, resets on reconnect
v0.15.1: Bug 13 improved — increased cave scan height - Bug 13: Increased IsNearSurface scan height from 20 to 64 blocks - Tall caves (17+ blocks) were fooling the scan — ceiling not found within 20 blocks - 64 blocks covers all realistic Stationeers terrain depths - Ore in tall natural caves and player-dug caverns now correctly rejected
v0.15.0: Bug 14 — Stale queue fix - Bug 14: Queue now clears when AIMeE is picked up, turned on, or given TargetXYZ - Prevents stale ore entries pointing to old locations after relocation - Frees AllAimeeQueuedMinables hash entries so other AIMeEs can claim ore - Detected via UpdateEachFrame state tracking (IsCursor, OnOff transitions) - TargetX/Y/Z writes detected via SetLogicValue postfix
v0.14.0: Bug 12 improved — direct SyncList injection - Bug 12: Replaced density-passthrough with direct VoxelChangeEvent SyncList injection - Bypasses SetDensityWorldSpace entirely — zero server-side terrain work - No octree modification, no UpdateCanAirPass, no DirtyLods on server - Client receives event, calls MineAtPositionClient — ore model disappears - Cleaner, faster, and no voxel density changes anywhere
v0.13.0: Bug 12 proper fix — density-passthrough approach - Bug 12: Ghost ore now disappears on clients WITHOUT modifying terrain - Reads current voxel density at ore position and re-sets it to same value - Creates VoxelChangeEvent (client sync) without changing any terrain - For surface ore (density < 50%): sync triggers MineAtPositionClient on clients - For buried ore (density >= 50%): invisible to players, self-heals on terrain mining or rejoin - No more terrain destruction from v0.12.0
v0.12.2: CRITICAL — Remove all terrain voxel modification - Removed ALL SetDensityWorldSpace calls — AIMeE must never modify terrain - Bug 12 (ghost ore) patch DISABLED — the game's client sync mechanism (VoxelChangeEvent) inherently modifies terrain density, which destroys the ground under/around ore - AIMeE only picks up surface ore via TryMineServer, no terrain digging - Ghost ore on clients is cosmetic; terrain destruction is gameplay-breaking
v0.12.1: Cliff-climbing regression fix - Fix: AIMeE can climb steep terrain again — velocity-based stuck detection now also checks position delta - Stuck detection only triggers reverse if position moved < 0.5m over 5 seconds (rules out slow climbing) - Previously, climbing a cliff at low velocity falsely triggered stuck→reverse loop every 5 seconds
v0.12.0: Bug 12 re-fix + Roam distance improvement - Bug 12: Multi-pronged ghost ore fix — calls SetDensityWorldSpace in OnMinedOre postfix (catches all mining paths) AND before TryMineServer in Roam prefix (matches Quarry pattern) - Bug 12: TryMineServer postfix kept as belt-and-suspenders with unconditional logging - Bug 12: Uses TargetMinable position via GetMinableWorldPosition for correct voxel coords - Roam: AIMeE now drives straight for 8-12 seconds before turning (was 1-3s) - Roam: Steering angle kept at ±30° for good coverage when turning
v0.11.0: Bug 12 + Bug 13 — Ghost ore fix + underground ore fix - Bug 12: Ore mined by AIMeE now correctly disappears on multiplayer clients - Bug 12: Postfix on Vein.TryMineServer adds SetDensityWorldSpace(0) for VoxelChangeEvent client sync - Bug 13: AIMeE no longer targets ore deep underground in tunnels/caves - Bug 13: Replaced single-voxel surface check with column scan that detects cave ceilings - Bug 13: Scans up to 20 blocks above ore; rejects if solid→empty→solid pattern found - Bug 7: Vicinity count now also uses improved Bug 13 surface check
v0.10.0: Bug 12 — Ghost ore client sync fix - Bug 12: Ore mined by AIMeE now correctly disappears on multiplayer clients - Bug 12: Postfix on Vein.TryMineServer adds SetDensityWorldSpace(0) to create VoxelChangeEvent for SyncList client sync - Bug 12: Also fixes the same base game bug in the original Roam() and async Mine() paths
v0.9.0: Bug 11 — Circling fix + debug overlay client/server awareness - Bug 11: Add post-reverse cooldown (3s) to break stuck→reverse→stuck feedback loops - Bug 11: Reset steering to mild angle (±5°) when reverse ends (was keeping ±30°) - Bug 11: Increase stuck detection time from 3s to 5s (less aggressive triggering) - Bug 11: Reduce velocity-stuck reverse duration from 5s to 3s - Bug 9: Reduce backward impulse from 2.0 to 1.0 (prevents wheelies) - Bug 9: Reduce random torque from ±3 to ±2 (less violent spin) - Bug 9: Reduce Unstuck reverse time from 8s to 5s - Queue cap reduced from 10 to 5 per AIMeE (better multi-AIMeE ore sharing) - Debug overlay now shows [SERVER] or [REMOTE] label per AIMeE - Client overlay shows only available data (vicinity, velocity, mode) - Client overlay explains that queue/target/motor are server-only
v0.8.0: Bug 9 + Bug 10 — Stuck teleport fix + closest ore selection - Bug 9: Replace Unstuck() teleport with physics-based escape (backward impulse + random torque) - Bug 9: Add velocity-based stuck detection in Roam (catches AIMeEs pushing against each other) - Bug 9: Increase obstacle raycast from 0.2m to 0.5m for earlier obstacle detection - Bug 9: Randomize steering angle on reverse to break stuck loops - Bug 10: Sort ore queue by distance — closest ore mined first - Bug 10: Distance sorting applied before queue cap, so farthest ore is dropped - Debug overlay now shows reversing state (Rev field)
v0.7.0: Bug 8 — Carry collision launch fix - Disable WheelColliders when AIMeE is being carried/dragged - Prevents suspension spring and friction forces from launching the player - Normal body collisions with walls/terrain still work as expected - Zero residual motor/brake torque on pickup for clean drop behavior - Call base DynamicThing.PhysicsUpdate for position tracking while carried
v0.6.1: Silent operation by default - Gate ALL runtime BepInEx logging behind aimeedebug toggle - No console output unless debug overlay is explicitly enabled - Startup/init messages still log once on load
v0.6.0: Vicinity diagnostic overhaul - Fix overlay showing different MineablesInVicinity than IC chip reads - Overlay now reads via GetLogicValue (same path as IC) for consistency - Add Bug7 call tracking: success/error counts visible in overlay - Add GetLogicValue(MineablesInVicinity) diagnostic postfix logging - Show both IC and direct vicinity values with MISMATCH detection - Log vicinity count on mode changes for diagnosing premature returns - Track vicinity changes in state change detection - More frequent Bug7 diagnostics (every 5s instead of 10s) - Always log Bug7 errors immediately (no more throttling)
v0.5.0: Debug overlay system - Add "aimeedebug" console command + F8 keyboard toggle - Floating text above each AIMeE with stats (mode, queue, vicinity, target, etc.) - Global debug panel (AIMeE count, AllAimeeQueuedMinables, time) - State change detection with logging (mode, queue, mining, target, storage) - Queue fill diagnostics with leak detection and emergency clear - Mode 3 periodic logging every 0.5s - Cached at 2Hz for minimal FPS impact
v0.4.2: Queue estimate cap fix - Cap MineablesInQueue client estimate to max 10 (queue cap)
v0.4.1: MineablesInQueue client fallback restored - Re-enable Bug 6 patch for MineablesInQueue on non-authority clients - Return capped queue estimate (max 10) instead of hard-zero on clients - Keep dedicated-server authority behavior unchanged (real queue still server-side)
v0.4.0: Full Roam() overhaul + multi-AIMeE fairness - Replace Bug 4 postfix with full Roam() prefix replacement - Hybrid old/new flow: queue system + always-drive (never stops on empty queue) - Cap queue to 10 per AIMeE to prevent AllAimeeQueuedMinables starvation - Use exact game motor/brake formulas (RocketMath.MapToScale) - Call original MovingToMineable() for mining state machine - Mine at position using game's exact TryMineServer flow with queue dequeue
v0.3.0: Bug 7 enabled, Bug 6 removed, roam fix - Enable Bug 7 MineablesInVicinity bounds-check (was incorrectly disabled) - Remove Bug 6 fake queue proxy that made MineablesInQueue == MineablesInVicinity - Improve Bug 4 roam exploration: longer steering persistence (4-8s) to reduce circling - Keep MinableSearchArea restored to 32 (original value when AIMeE worked well)
v0.2.3: Bug 7 fix + Bug 6 ambiguous method fix - Fix MineablesInVicinity overcounting ore outside search bounds (Bug 7) - Fix GetLogicValue ambiguous match error from two-overload resolution (Bug 6)
v0.2.2: Bug 6 fix - Fix MineablesInQueue always 0 on multiplayer clients (Bug 6)
v0.2.1: Bug 5 fix - Fix picking up AIMeE launching player into the air (Bug 5) - Changed UpdateEachFrame from prefix to postfix to preserve view-following
v0.2.0: Bugs 3 and 4 - Fix tread UV animation on multiplayer clients via WheelRpm estimation from velocity (Bug 3) - Fix server-side AnimateAuthority skipped when occluded (Bug 3) - Fix Roam regression: restore driving logic when ore queue is empty (Bug 4) - Restore MinableSearchArea from 16 to 32 (Bug 4)
v0.1.0: Initial release - Fix mine deadlock: _mineCancellation reset after Mine completes (Bug 1) - Fix off-state teleporting: skip TryUnstuck/PathToTarget when off (Bug 2)
ModMetadata.InGameDescription:
<size="30"><color=#ffa500>AIMeE Bug Fixes</color></size> <color=#ff0000>This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx with StationeersLaunchPad.</color>
Fixes fourteen AIMeE mining robot bugs:
<color=#ffa500>1. Mine Deadlock</color> AIMeE stops after first ore because the mining cancellation token is never reset.
<color=#ffa500>2. Off-State Teleporting</color> When off but receiving logic, AIMeE hops/teleports due to missing power guards.
<color=#ffa500>3. Tread Animation Desync</color> Tread animations frozen on multiplayer clients. WheelRpm estimated from synced velocity.
<color=#ffa500>4. Roam Regression</color> AIMeE stops exploring when no ore nearby. Driving logic restored when queue empty. Scan range restored to 32.
<color=#ffa500>5. Pickup Launch</color> Picking up AIMeE launches the player. Motor/brakes zeroed while dragged.
<color=#ffa500>6. MineablesInQueue (Multiplayer)</color> Always 0 on clients. Uses terrain query as fallback for non-authority.
<color=#ffa500>7. MineablesInVicinity Overcount</color> Counts ore beyond search area. Added per-ore bounds check matching queue logic.
<color=#ffa500>8. Carry Collision Launch</color> Carrying AIMeE near terrain/objects launches player. WheelColliders disabled while carried.
<color=#ffa500>9. Stuck Teleporting</color> AIMeE teleports when stuck for 60s. Replaced with physics-based reverse + random turn.
<color=#ffa500>10. Distant Ore Selection</color> AIMeE mines far-away ore first. Queue now sorted by distance — closest ore mined first.
<color=#ffa500>11. Circling Fix</color> AIMeEs circle aimlessly due to stuck detection feedback loop. Post-reverse cooldown and steering reset break the cycle. Queue cap reduced for multi-AIMeE fairness.
<color=#ffa500>12. Ghost Ore Fix (Multiplayer)</color> Ore mined by AIMeE stays visible on clients. Fixed by injecting sync event directly — client removes the ore model. Zero terrain modification.
<color=#ffa500>13. Underground Ore Fix</color> AIMeE targets ore deep underground in tunnels/caves. Column scan now detects cave ceilings and rejects unreachable ore.
<color=#ffa500>14. Stale Queue Fix</color> AIMeE's ore queue isn't cleared on pickup, power-on, or TargetXYZ set. Stale entries waste time and block other AIMeEs. Queue now resets on relocation.
<color=#888888>Debug overlay shows [SERVER]/[REMOTE] labels. On dedicated server clients, server-only values (queue, target, motor) display as unavailable.</color>
